key_insight: dpti automates thermodynamic integration workflows so phase diagrams can be computed efficiently from machine learning interatomic potentials.
engineering_relevance: It reduces the manual effort and complexity of free-energy and coexistence calculations for solid–liquid phase boundaries in computational materials design.
method: Builds reversible integration paths from analytically known reference systems to MLIP-described phases.
Automates generation and execution of the required MD tasks from JSON inputs.
Computes free-energy contributions, estimates errors, and propagates coexistence points into phase boundaries.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports two demonstration cases with Deep Potential models: a silica phase diagram and the ice Ih–liquid water boundary.
watch_item: The workflow still depends on carefully designed reversible paths and many MD tasks, so setup quality and path choice can affect accuracy and robustness.
paper_title: dpti: An Automated Thermodynamic Integration Workflow for Phase Diagram Calculations with Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
